# Codebase Report: Portal (Bridge) - Architecture, Sessions, Workspace, and Layout Systems
Generated: 2026-02-27

## Summary

"Portal" is a React Native (Expo) mobile app named **Bridge** in the README. It is a terminal session manager and DevOps dashboard that connects to remote hosts running a small Node.js agent. The app lets users manage tmux sessions, Docker containers, run commands, and view system metrics -- all from a mobile device. There is NO "niri" tiling window manager code anywhere in the codebase. The workspace/screen system in the app is the app's OWN niri-INSPIRED layout: a 2D pager grid where vertical axis = workspaces, horizontal axis = windows within each workspace.

---

## Project Structure

```
portal/
├── app/                         # Expo Router screens (file-based routing)
│   ├── (tabs)/                  # Bottom tab navigator (hidden tab bar)
│   │   ├── _layout.tsx          # Tab config: Sessions, Hosts, Projects, More
│   │   ├── index.tsx            # HOME SCREEN - the main workspace pager
│   │   ├── hosts.tsx            # Host list tab
│   │   ├── projects.tsx         # Projects tab
│   │   └── more.tsx             # Settings/more tab
│   ├── session/[hostId]/[name]/ # Session detail routes
│   │   ├── index.tsx            # Session info/rename/kill screen
│   │   └── terminal.tsx         # Standalone full-screen terminal
│   ├── hosts/[id]/              # Host detail routes
│   │   ├── index.tsx            # Host detail
│   │   ├── edit.tsx             # Edit host
│   │   └── docker/[containerId] # Docker container detail
│   ├── projects/                # Project routes
│   ├── ports/                   # Port forwarding routes
│   └── _layout.tsx              # Root layout (providers, navigation stack)
│
├── components/
│   ├── workspace/               # THE CORE WORKSPACE SYSTEM
│   │   ├── WorkspacePager.tsx   # Horizontal pager for windows in one workspace
│   │   ├── WorkspaceIndicator.tsx # Right-side dot indicator (tap/drag to switch)
│   │   ├── SessionTerminalPage.tsx # Terminal window page
│   │   ├── LaunchpadPage.tsx    # Empty/last page with launch actions
│   │   ├── BrowserPage.tsx      # In-workspace browser window
│   │   ├── WindowPage.tsx       # Wraps tab screens as windows (hosts, projects, settings)
│   │   └── SessionPickerSheet.tsx # UNUSED - session picker bottom sheet (dead code)
│   └── [many UI components]     # Cards, forms, modals, etc.
│
├── lib/
│   ├── workspace-types.ts       # Window, Workspace, SessionWithHost types
│   ├── useWorkspaceState.ts     # THE MAIN STATE HOOK for the 2D workspace
│   ├── store.tsx                # Global app state (hosts, preferences)
│   ├── types.ts                 # Domain types (Host, Session, Docker, etc.)
│   ├── live.tsx                 # WebSocket live polling hooks
│   ├── launch-sheet.tsx         # Global LaunchSheet context (open/close)
│   ├── projects-store.tsx       # Projects + recent launches
│   └── [other lib files]
│
├── agent/                       # Node.js server that runs ON managed hosts
│   └── src/http/routes/         # REST API routes (sessions, docker, files, etc.)
│
└── thoughts/                    # Design docs and handoff notes
    ├── handoffs/                # Claude handoff task files
    ├── shared/plans/            # Feature plans (PLAN-*.md)
    └── terminal-layout-debug.md # Terminal sizing debugging history
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Framework | React Native 0.81.5 + Expo 54 |
| Router | Expo Router (file-based, Stack + Tabs) |
| State | React Context (useStore, ProjectsProvider, etc.) + AsyncStorage |
| Data fetching | TanStack Query v5 |
| Gestures | react-native-gesture-handler |
| Paging | react-native-pager-view (native PagerView) |
| Terminal | xterm.js in a WebView (TerminalWebView.tsx) |
| Icons | lucide-react-native |
| Animations | react-native-reanimated + Animated API |
| Agent | Node.js + Hono (runs on managed hosts) |
| Language | TypeScript |

---

## Questions Answered

### Q1: What kind of app is this?

Bridge is a **mobile DevOps terminal manager**. It connects to remote Linux servers running the Bridge agent (a Node.js/Hono HTTP+WebSocket server). Features:
- Multiple host profiles with auth tokens
- Live tmux session list (create/rename/kill)
- Full terminal emulator (xterm.js via WebView, WebSocket to agent)
- Docker container management
- GitHub CI status monitoring
- Project launching with recent launches history
- Port/tunnel management
- Live activity and push notifications

---

### Q2: How sessions/screens are managed

**Session = a tmux session on a remote host** (type: `Session` in `lib/types.ts`).

**Screen/Window = a page in the workspace pager** (type: `Window` in `lib/workspace-types.ts`).

The core flow:
1. `useHostsLive()` polls the agent every 2 seconds for live session data
2. `useWorkspaceState(allSessions)` manages the 2D workspace layout
3. The home screen (`app/(tabs)/index.tsx`) renders the 2D pager

**Window routes** (what can be a workspace window):
- `terminal` - a tmux session terminal (params: hostId, sessionName)
- `browser` - an in-app web browser (params: url)
- `hosts` / `projects` / `settings` - the corresponding tab screens embedded as windows

**Workspace state** (`lib/useWorkspaceState.ts`):
- Persisted to AsyncStorage under key `bridge.workspaces.v1`
- Auto-assigns new sessions to the active workspace
- Removes dead sessions (ones no longer reported by the agent)
- Supports multiple workspaces, each with multiple windows

---

### Q3: "Niri" references

There is **no "niri" code** in this codebase. Zero occurrences of the word. The workspace system is the developer's OWN implementation inspired by niri's 2D tiling concept (a Wayland compositor). The naming "workspace" + the 2D pager structure is the spiritual equivalent.

The system:
- **Vertical axis** = workspaces (switch by 2-finger vertical swipe or WorkspaceIndicator)
- **Horizontal axis** = windows within a workspace (swipe left/right with PagerView)
- **Overview mode** = pinch-to-zoom shows all workspaces and windows as scaled cards (like a macOS Expose / niri overview)

---

### Q4: "Old" vs "New" screen format/layout systems

Based on git history and the terminal-layout-debug.md file, there WAS an old layout system:

**Old system (removed):**
- Transform-based pager using `Animated.View` + translateX transforms
- `ScrollView` with `pagingEnabled` as intermediate step
- Custom horizontal scroll with explicit width/height calculations
- Problems: xterm.js `getBoundingClientRect` was unreliable with CSS transforms; caused terminal sizing bugs

**Current/New system:**
- `react-native-pager-view` (native `PagerView` component)
- Vertical `PagerView` (orientation="vertical") for workspaces
- Horizontal `PagerView` (default) for windows within each workspace
- This eliminates the CSS transform / getBoundingClientRect problem

The workspace system itself was the "new" addition (not just terminal paging):
- The workspace + window abstraction (Window, Workspace types) is new
- The 2D navigation (vertical for workspaces, horizontal for windows) is new
- The overview mode (pinch-to-zoom, scrollview of cards) is new
- Browser windows within workspaces are new

---

### Q5: Entry points and how a session starts

**App Entry:**
1. `app/_layout.tsx` - Root layout, mounts providers (GestureHandler, SafeArea, Query, Store, Theme, LaunchSheet)
2. `app/(tabs)/index.tsx` - The home tab (Sessions screen) is the primary UI

**Session Lifecycle:**
1. User taps "New Session" button (LaunchSheet opens via `useLaunchSheet()`)
2. `LaunchSheet` component selects host + command + project and calls the agent REST API
3. Agent creates the tmux session
4. `useHostsLive()` polling picks up the new session in ~2 seconds
5. `useWorkspaceState` auto-assigns the new session to the active workspace as a new terminal Window
6. `WorkspacePager` renders `SessionTerminalPage` for that window
7. `SessionTerminalPage` builds the xterm.js HTML with a WebSocket URL pointing to the agent
8. `TerminalWebView` loads the HTML, xterm.js connects via WebSocket, terminal is live

**Navigation to existing session terminal:**
- From session detail screen (`app/session/[hostId]/[name]/index.tsx`), tap "Terminal" button
- This goes to `app/session/[hostId]/[name]/terminal.tsx` (standalone full-screen terminal, NOT in workspace pager)
- The workspace pager approach is the primary/new way; the standalone terminal route exists as a secondary access path

---

## Dead/Unused Code

| Component/File | Status | Evidence |
|----------------|--------|---------|
| `components/workspace/SessionPickerSheet.tsx` | UNUSED - defined but never imported | grep shows zero imports outside the file itself |
| `app/session/[hostId]/[name]/index.tsx` | Likely legacy - session detail/management screen | The workspace system bypasses this; sessions are managed inline |
| The `(tabs)` tab bar itself | Hidden - `display: 'none'` in tabBarStyle | The tabs are accessible as WindowPage routes, not via a visible tab bar |

---

## Architecture Map

```
[Remote Host] ←WebSocket→ [TerminalWebView (xterm.js)]
      ↕ REST API
[Agent (Node.js/Hono)] ──polls── [Bridge App]
                                        │
                               [useHostsLive()]
                                        │
                               [useWorkspaceState()]
                                        │
                          ┌────────────────────────────┐
                          │  app/(tabs)/index.tsx       │
                          │  (Home Screen)              │
                          │                             │
                          │  Vertical PagerView         │
                          │  ┌─────────────────────┐   │
                          │  │ Workspace 0          │   │
                          │  │  Horizontal Pager    │   │
                          │  │  [Terminal][Browser] │   │
                          │  │  [LaunchpadPage]     │   │
                          │  └─────────────────────┘   │
                          │  ┌─────────────────────┐   │
                          │  │ Workspace 1          │   │
                          │  │  Horizontal Pager    │   │
                          │  │  [Terminal][Hosts]   │   │
                          │  └─────────────────────┘   │
                          │  [Empty Workspace slot]     │
                          │                             │
                          │  [WorkspaceIndicator dots]  │
                          │  [Overview mode (pinch)]    │
                          └────────────────────────────┘
```

## Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Exports |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `app/(tabs)/index.tsx` | Main home screen, orchestrates workspace pager | `SessionsScreen` |
| `lib/useWorkspaceState.ts` | Workspace state, persistence, reconciliation | `useWorkspaceState()` |
| `lib/workspace-types.ts` | Type definitions for windows/workspaces | `Window`, `Workspace`, `SessionWithHost` |
| `components/workspace/WorkspacePager.tsx` | Horizontal pager for one workspace | `WorkspacePager` |
| `components/workspace/WorkspaceIndicator.tsx` | Right-side dot nav, tap/drag gestures | `WorkspaceIndicator` |
| `components/workspace/SessionTerminalPage.tsx` | Terminal page (xterm.js WebView + keyboard bar) | `SessionTerminalPage` |
| `components/workspace/LaunchpadPage.tsx` | Last page in pager - new session/browser/nav | `LaunchpadPage` |
| `components/workspace/BrowserPage.tsx` | In-workspace browser window | `BrowserPage` |
| `components/workspace/WindowPage.tsx` | Wraps tab screens (hosts/projects/settings) as windows | `WindowPage` |
| `components/workspace/SessionPickerSheet.tsx` | UNUSED bottom sheet | `SessionPickerSheet` |
| `lib/store.tsx` | Global hosts + preferences state | `useStore()`, `StoreProvider` |
| `lib/types.ts` | All domain types | `Host`, `Session`, `DockerContainer`, etc. |
| `lib/live.tsx` | WebSocket live polling hooks | `useHostsLive()`, `useHostLive()` |
| `lib/launch-sheet.tsx` | LaunchSheet open/close context | `useLaunchSheet()` |
| `app/_layout.tsx` | Root layout with all providers | `RootLayout` |
